writeup: The Buffalo Bills have settled into the calm stretch of the NFL calendar, with the frenetic activity of free agency and the draft now firmly in the rearview mirror. As noted by Buffalo Rumblings, this is traditionally the quietest portion of the season, a brief pause before the intensity of training camp ramps up. For Bills fans, this period offers a chance to reflect on the roster construction and anticipate what the team will look like when pads go on. With Josh Allen and company set to resume on-field work in the coming weeks, the silence feels more like the calm before a storm than any cause for concern.

articles.title: The Bills Have Entered the Quietest Portion of the 2026 NFL Season
